misconfig_patterns: access_authorization defaults to False on bare `bentoml serve` deployments
Yatai dashboard /setup?token=<TOKEN> claimable if exposed (16-char alphanum default)
Pickle deserialization RCE chain (CVE-2026-44345, CVE-2026-44346) on pre-1.4.39 versions
Path traversal CVE history; older bento package bundles may include backdoored artifacts
/docs and /docs.json publicly readable by default; exposes full route surface
/metrics Prometheus endpoint unauth — model performance + queue depth leakage

active_probe.false_positive_check: /docs.json must include OpenAPI spec with x-bentoml-name extension or info.contact.email containing bentoml.com

deployment_tells: HTTP response header 'Server: BentoML Service/<svc_name>' is pathognomonic
X-BentoML-Request-ID and X-BentoML-Trace-ID headers on every response
info.title in /docs.json often matches the service class name
Yatai admin UI on port 8080 with /setup?token= claim flow
pivot_paths: GET / -> Swagger UI with route shape preview (identity surface per Insight #78)
POST <inference_route> with model-shaped input -> credential pool + model identity (auth-bearing; restraint ethic gates)
GET /metrics -> Prometheus model perf + queue depth (information leak even if benign)
GET /setup on port 8080 -> Yatai admin claim surface (if not yet initialized)
vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-44345 pickle deserialization RCE chain via malicious bento package (patched in 1.4.39)
CVE-2026-44346 path traversal in bento bundle resolver (patched in 1.4.39)
14 total NVD CVEs across BentoML history, RCE + path traversal dominant
sources: bentoml/BentoML GitHub repo (8,670 stars, Apache-2.0, Python, pushed 2026-06-03)
